title: senecio kunturinus
intro: Senecio kunturinus, also known as Andean wormwood, is a plant species in the daisy family native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for digestive issues and fever reduction, due to its active compounds such as sesquiterpenes and diterpenes. The health benefits of Senecio kunturinus include relief from nausea, diarrhea, and headaches, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Herbal preparations of Senecio kunturinus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant medicinally.
